name: Fresh Rosemary Onion Focaccia ABM
recipeIngredient: 1 large Spanish onion, cooked in 2 tablespoons olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all dough ingredients except the cooked onion in the bread machine.
Program for knead and first rise. Press start.
2. Strain the onions (and their cooking liquid) and return the liquid to the machine.
3. At the end of the final knead, add the chopped onion (without liquid) to the machine.
Restart and knead only until the onion is roughly mixed in. The dough will be wet,
and the onion will remain in clumps, sticking out of the dough.
4. Turn the dough out onto a well-floured surface and knead briefly by hand
to form a ball.
5. Oil a pizza pan or baking sheet. Place the dough on the prepared pan
and pat into a 12-inch disk.
6. Coat the top of the dough with onion juices. Cover with plastic wrap.
Let it rise at room temperature until doubled in bulk, or refrigerate overnight.
7. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Position the rack in the center.
8. Just before baking, use your fingertips to gently make indentations
in the surface of the dough. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with coarse salt and ground pepper.
9. Bake for 10 minutes at 450°F, then reduce the temperature to 350°F.
Continue baking for another 12-15 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown.
10. Serve hot or at room temperature.
